quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 6 Mixed Review
quiz.subtitle: Unit 6 Quiz 11: Exponential & Logarithmic Functions тАФ Challenging Problems (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Unit 6: Exponential & Logarithmic Functions тАФ High-End Mixed Review

questions.question: Solve $\log_3(x - 4) + \log_3(x + 2) = 2$.
questions.explanation: Combine: $\log_3((x-4)(x+2)) = 2$ тЖТ $(x-4)(x+2) = 9$ тЖТ $x^2 - 2x - 8 = 9$ тЖТ $x^2 - 2x - 17 = 0$. Quadratic formula: $x = 1 \pm 3\sqrt{2}$. Domain requires $x > 4$, so only $x = 1 + 3\sqrt{2} \approx 5.24$ is valid.

questions.question: Solve $\ln(x + 3) + \ln(x - 1) = \ln(15)$.
questions.explanation: Combine: $\ln((x+3)(x-1)) = \ln(15)$ тЖТ $(x+3)(x-1) = 15$ тЖТ $x^2 + 2x - 3 = 15$ тЖТ $x^2 + 2x - 18 = 0$. Solve: $x = -1 \pm \sqrt{19}$. Domain requires $x > 1$, so only $x = -1 + \sqrt{19} \approx 3.36$ is valid.

questions.question: Solve $e^{2x} - 5e^x + 6 = 0$.
questions.explanation: Let $u = e^x$: $u^2 - 5u + 6 = 0$ тЖТ $(u-2)(u-3) = 0$. $u = 2$ тЖТ $e^x = 2$ тЖТ $x = \ln 2$. $u = 3$ тЖТ $e^x = 3$ тЖТ $x = \ln 3$. This is a quadratic in exponential form.

questions.question: Solve $2^{x+1} > 32$.
questions.explanation: $32 = 2^5$. So $2^{x+1} > 2^5$. Since the base $2 > 1$, the inequality is preserved: $x+1 > 5$ тЖТ $x > 4$.

questions.question: Solve $\log_4(x + 12) - \log_4(x - 3) = 1$.
questions.explanation: Condense: $\log_4(\frac{x+12}{x-3}) = 1$ тЖТ $\frac{x+12}{x-3} = 4$ тЖТ $x+12 = 4x-12$ тЖТ $3x = 24$ тЖТ $x = 8$. Check: $\log_4(20) - \log_4(5) = \log_4(4) = 1$. тЬУ

questions.question: A substance decays according to $A(t) = A_0 e^{-kt}$. If 40% remains after 10 days, find $k$ to four decimal places. Use $\ln 0.4 \approx -0.9163$.
Solve $5^{x} = 3^{x+2}$. Give the exact answer using logarithms.
questions.explanation: Take log of both sides: $x\log 5 = (x+2)\log 3$. Expand: $x\log 5 = x\log 3 + 2\log 3$. Isolate $x$: $x(\log 5 - \log 3) = 2\log 3$ тЖТ $x = \frac{2\log 3}{\log(5/3)}$. Use logarithms when bases can't be made the same.

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered challenging exponential and logarithmic problems: solving equations with different bases, log equations that lead to quadratics, exponentials requiring substitution, condensing and evaluating with given values, inequalities, half-life decay, and equations requiring logarithms.
scoreMessages.70: Strong work! Key areas to review: (1) When combining logs, always check domain restrictions тАФ log arguments must be positive, (2) For equations like $e^{2x} - 5e^x + 6 = 0$, substitute $u = e^x$ to get a quadratic, (3) When bases can't be equal, take the log of both sides and isolate $x$.
scoreMessages.50: Good effort on challenging material. Focus on: (1) Rewriting both sides with the same base when possible, (2) Using log properties to condense multiple logs into one before converting to exponential form, (3) For exponential equations with different bases that can't be made equal, use logarithms: $a^x = b^y$ тЖТ $x\log a = y\log b$.
scoreMessages.0: Review advanced exponential and logarithmic techniques: (1) $b^{f(x)} = b^{g(x)}$ тЖТ $f(x) = g(x)$, (2) $\log_b(f(x)) = c$ тЖТ $f(x) = b^c$, (3) Combine logs: $\log_b(M) \pm \log_b(N) = \log_b(MN)$ or $\log_b(M/N)$, (4) For different bases, take $\log$ or $\ln$ of both sides.
